architecture_fit.note: Single attention head (counting head + bilinear QK split score), no MLP, no Python routing — a minimal delta from base_model.py that hits ~1.0 split accuracy.

baseline_comparison.note: Uniform baseline (0.371) plus two named ablations (no-position 0.84, depth-ablated 0.55) clearly separate the mechanism from strawmen.

faithfulness.note: Hand-built circuit with genuine within-circuit ablations isolating each ingredient; explicitly flags the synthetic case and proposes trained-head patching, but has no trained model to causally verify.

operating_range.note: Flat ~1.0000 across every span length 3–8 (robustness 0.9999), the task's full available range, vs a declining uniform baseline.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Fully hand-set weights derived as an explicit feature map phi(k)=[D,D^2,k,kD]; no learning at all — strong evidence of mechanism understanding.

visual_judgement.note: Depth profile + attention-over-splits per cell type, plus headline+ablation bars and a per-span line — makes the single-head claim directly legible.

visualisation_rationale.note: README ties each panel to the claim: depth level vs attention mass, the three cell-type regimes, ablations isolating ingredients, span sweep against cherry-picking.

notes: An excellent hand-built attempt: one genuine single attention head with an explicit bilinear QK score solves all three filled-cell types with ~1.0 split accuracy, no MLP and no data-dependent routing — directly fixing pass_2's flagged hand-coded dispatch. Baseline plus two named ablations and a flat per-span sweep make a tight, legible argument. The one reservation is faithfulness: the causal evidence is ablation of the hand-built circuit itself rather than patching a trained model, which the README honestly acknowledges and addresses.
